changes_summary: Documented the missing milestone source file as a prompt-vs-repo mismatch, and added an explicit hook-safe disabled-mode bridge for `null`/`undefined` Join Clips project IDs using an adapter-local inert sentinel entity.

success_criteria.criterion: `createEntityStore` exposes the requested generic API, stays React Query-free, and provides one Zustand store per factory instance with per-entity state keyed by `entityId`.
The store implements atomic bootstrap, lazy per-entity load, optimistic updates, text-field flush, revert, reset, save, saveImmediate, and error handling without discarding optimistic local state.
The store uses an explicit clean-baseline model so `reset()` leaves an entity clean while `hasPersistedData` remains separately tracked.

The store exposes only minimal internal dirty/pending inspection and external-sync helpers needed for adapter lifecycle flushing and safe query reconciliation, without widening the public `EntityView<T>` contract beyond M1 needs.
`useEntity(entityId)` subscriptions are isolated so updates to one entity do not re-render another entity consumer.
`useJoinClipsSettings(projectId)` is ported to the store factory while preserving the full existing public hook contract used by current callers.
Join Clips keeps `useToolSettings` as the only authoritative merged-load and readiness source and does not introduce a second merged-settings read path in M1.
The plan remains aligned with the prompt brief despite the missing `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` file by treating the prompt and verified repo behavior as the milestone source of truth.

`useJoinClipsSettings.status` remains behavior-compatible with current callers by staying `loading` until the authoritative merged settings snapshot for the current project has been safely reconciled into the store.
`initializeFrom` remains a no-op in the React Query-backed Join Clips adapter, matching the current exported behavior.
`reset(newDefaults?)` preserves current clean-after-reset behavior by updating the adapter/store clean baseline so `isDirty` becomes false afterward.

The Join Clips adapter handles `null` and `undefined` `projectId` values without conditional hook calls or shared disabled-state leakage by using an adapter-local inert sentinel that is never exposed or persisted.

The Join Clips adapter keeps React Query behavior coherent by continuing to read merged settings through `useToolSettings` and explicitly synchronizing or invalidating the relevant settings cache after direct saves.
Join Clips preserves best-effort persistence of pending edits on project change, hook unmount, and browser unload, matching the lifecycle behavior it currently inherits from `useAutoSaveSettings`.
Join Clips prompt and textarea settings keep their current debounced autosave behavior in M1; no new blur-only wiring is silently introduced.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly after the refactor, covering downstream consumers typed against `ReturnType<typeof useJoinClipsSettings>`.

Targeted tests for `createEntityStore` and `useJoinClipsSettings` pass and cover bootstrap, update, flush, revert, reset-clean behavior, selector isolation, safe external reconciliation, status timing, contract-preserving `initializeFrom`, nullish-ID handling, query-sync behavior, and lifecycle flush behavior.
`npm test` returns to the stated M0 baseline with no regressions.
The final design remains readable shared infrastructure and does not turn M1 into a broader settings-runtime refactor.
M0 telemetry remains unchanged because no instrumented surface was intentionally modified in this milestone.

assumptions: This turn is revising the implementation plan rather than executing the code changes.
The milestone goal remains correct: establish the generic Zustand store factory and validate it by porting only `useJoinClipsSettings`.
`useJoinClipsSettings` must preserve current behavior, not just current types, because `JoinClipsPage` and `useClipManager` depend on its status semantics.
The prompt brief plus verified repository behavior are the source of truth for M1 because the referenced `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` file is not present in this checkout.
For this first consumer, avoiding a second merged-settings read path is more important than exercising the factory's generic `load()` capability against the existing merged settings runtime.
A module-local disabled sentinel entity is acceptable in the adapter as long as it is never exposed publicly, never performs real persistence work, and does not broaden the generic store API.
`zustand` may already be present in `package.json`; if so, the dependency task is verification and lockfile consistency rather than a meaningful functional change.
